exemplar.title: Building a Badminton App - Shuttle Up!

detailedExemplarData.title: Building a Badminton App - Shuttle Up!

strengths: Clear description of research methods, including surveys and interviews, which showcases strong information-literacy skills and grounds your design decisions in user feedback.
Effective use of OPVL to critically evaluate a key source, demonstrating advanced critical-thinking and source-evaluation abilities.
Illustration of perseverance and problem-solving through recounting a technical challenge (password storage) and your solution, highlighting affective skills.
Inclusion of a survey bar chart and data table as concrete evidence, reinforcing the relevance of your findings.
Well-structured Research Plan table with targeted questions for each feature, indicating purposeful application of research skills.
Detailed Self-Management section describing how you used action plans and reminder apps to improve organization and meet deadlines.
weaknesses: References to ATL skills are generalized (âCriterion Bâ); explicitly naming skills (e.g., information-literacy, self-management) and linking each to specific examples would strengthen the reflection on skill application.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your application of ATL skills is robust, with strong examples of research, critical thinking and self-management that directly support both your learning goals and the productâs development. To refine this further, make each ATL skill explicit and tie it to a concrete instance of your work, which will clearly demonstrate the impact of each skill on your process and outcomes.

strengths: Clear, personally meaningful learning goal that connects your badminton experience to specific physical, technical and mental skills, enhancing motivation and focus.
Comprehensive product goal outlining target users, core features and rationale, which provides a solid foundation for development and stakeholder clarity.
Systematic use of the ACCESSFM framework to structure success criteria, ensuring you address multiple dimensions of your design.
Detailed action plan with clear dates, ownership and status updates, demonstrating effective time management and accountability.
Reflection on a major setback (video filming schedule) and subsequent plan adjustment, showing flexibility and realistic project management.
weaknesses: Learning goal is stated broadly and would benefit from specific, measurable targets (for example, âmaster 5 key drills with 80% accuracyâ).
Several success criteria lack quantitative metrics (for example, defining âdiverse collectionâ or âmodern designâ); adding numbers or thresholds would enable objective evaluation.
The extended ACCESSFM table is thorough but could be improved by prioritising and sequencing criteria, aligning resources to the most critical requirements first.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Overall, your planning demonstrates strong organization and personal engagement. You have clearly defined what you want to achieve and how you will get there, supported by a structured framework and a realistic schedule. To reach the highest level, focus on making your goals and success criteria measurable and on ordering your priorities to ensure efficient use of time and resources.

strengths: Inclusion of a product interface screenshot paired with a narrative gives concrete visual evidence for your evaluation, making your reflection more vivid and credible.
Thorough evaluation of a core success criterion against user survey data, exemplifying evidence-based reflection that aligns with top standards.
Comprehensive table assessing criteria such as security, cost and navigation, showing detailed measurement against your success criteria.
weaknesses: Final reflection lacks explicit discussion of how this project informs your future IB studies or professional goals; linking your achievements to long-term growth would deepen the reflection.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Your reflective practice is strong, with detailed, evidence-based evaluations of the product against success criteria and clear insights into what worked well. To elevate your reflection, discuss how the skills and experiences gained will influence your future academic or professional endeavors, thereby closing the loop between this project and your ongoing learning journey.

annotations.comment: You establish a clear, personally meaningful learning goal, connecting your badminton experience to specific physical, technical and mental skill areas. This strong personal context enhances motivation and focus.
Your learning goal is broad; consider specifying measurable outcomes (e.g. master 5 key drills with 80% accuracy) to track progress and demonstrate achievement concretely.
You provide a comprehensive product goal, detailing target users, core features and rationale. This clarity supports focused development and stakeholder understanding.

Several success criteria lack quantitative targets (e.g., âdiverse collectionâ or âmodern designâ). Define specific metrics (number of videos, load-time thresholds) to enable objective evaluation.
Your use of ACCESSFM to structure success criteria shows systematic planning and ensures you consider diverse dimensions of your product design.
The extended ACCESSFM table covers many aspects but would benefit from prioritisation and criteria sequencing to align resources with your most critical requirements.
Your action plan is detailed, with clear dates, ownership and status. This demonstrates effective planning, accountability and time management.
You reflect on a major setback (video filming schedule) and adjust your plan accordingly. This shows flexibility and realistic project management.
You describe your research methods clearly, demonstrating strong information-literacy skills. Citing survey and interview data provides evidence of effective knowledge gathering.
Using OPVL to evaluate a key source demonstrates critical-thinking and source-evaluation skills, enhancing the credibility of your research foundation.
You recount a significant technical challenge (password storage) and how perseverance and problem-solving led to a solution, demonstrating effective affective skills.
You reference âCriterion Bâ and research but could strengthen by explicitly naming ATL skills (e.g., information-literacy, self-management) and linking each to a concrete example.
Including your surveyâs bar-chart and data table shows strong evidence use. This effectively grounds your design decisions in user feedback.
Your Research Plan table shows thoughtful, targeted questions for each feature, illustrating purposeful application of research skills to inform app content.
Your Self-Management section clearly describes how action plans and reminder apps improved your organization. This is solid evidence of growth in that ATL skill.
Your inclusion of a product interface screenshot and narrative under Criteria C provides concrete visual evidence for your evaluation, strengthening your reflection.
Evaluating your core success criterion against user survey data demonstrates thorough, evidence-based reflection that meets the highest standard of Criterion C.
Your detailed table on criteria like security, cost and navigation clearly shows how you measured each aspect, supporting a comprehensive product evaluation.
Your concluding reflection is strong but could include more explicit discussion of how achieving this project informs your future IB or professional goals.
